extra.reason: Browser Setlist editor (// 05) shipped in PR #20: ordered+shuffle modes, weighted with-replacement sampling, repeat-as-burst, gap/avoid-tag/mood-arc/seed rules, active-on-boot flag, full transport with dry-run, plus seq-step delete button and bake timeout bumps. Verified live on 3-board cluster.

Fix committed to codex/servo-iga-motion-editor branch (commit 28ed49f). 4px drag threshold preserves keyframe DOM identity through stationary clicks so dblclick fires; pointerup no-ops when no drag occurred; keyIndex tracked through normalizeKeyframes resorts during drag. Lands on main when PR #21 merges.

Motion editor // 06 shipped in PR #21: 12-track cluster authoring (3 servo + 1 DC per board), click-add/drag-move/dblclick-delete keyframes, snap with shift-override, onion-skin previous keyframe, per-track solo/mute, collapsible board headers with physical IP labels, live transport with Loop, inflight=1+coalesce send model that fixed the erratic playback issue, plus the easing-preservation fix from CodeRabbit. Verified live on 3-board cluster.

Superseded: operator decided Serial Monitor + CAL_* commands are sufficient for the gallery cluster (all servos identical). No browser // 08 calibration UI needed. Firmware calibration storage + Serial commands shipping via servo-2n9 (PR #22).

Shipped in PR #22 (cf6e11c on main). Persisted calibration via Serial CAL_GET/SET/RESET/PULSE commands, 16-byte EEPROM record at offset 8176, range-validated inputs and defensive calibrationGet, applyToServoConfig integrates with degreesToPulse via new offsetDeg field. HTTP /calibration route dropped pre-merge (operator decided Serial Monitor is sufficient — servo-ehd closed as superseded). +3888 OTA budget remaining.

Shipped in PR #23 (ce89ba0 on main). Servo tracks switched to 0..100% down semantic, dispatched via DOWN <ch> <pct> through firmware's percentToDegrees + calibrated up/downDegrees; DC tracks step between keyframes, servo tracks ramp; legacy 0..180 degree motions auto-migrate on load; 15% value-axis inset prevents adjacent-row diamond overlap; pre-roll glide from last-known position to motion start (dispatch-completion exit). Follow-ups filed: servo-l8l (firmware MOTION engine clamp 0..180 for baked playback), servo-0bt (HTTP HOL blocking transport).

Fixed by USB reflash on the affected board. The underlying DHCP race may still exist in firmware but didn't reproduce after a clean USB flash — most likely the OTA partition was in a half-applied state and the USB flash cleared it. Will reopen if it surfaces again.
Shipped in PR #24 (c2bbebd on main). downDegrees=1800 for ch 0/1/2 — DOWN <ch> 0 → DOWN <ch> 100 now sweeps the full 5-turn extent on the goBILDA winches.

Superseded by servo-79q. After per-segment DMOVE dispatch lands, motion playback sends ~1 HTTP per segment instead of ~5/sec, so HTTP/1.1 head-of-line blocking is no longer a meaningful constraint on the user-visible 'smooth playback' goal. If specific scrub-rate, pre-roll glide, or non-linear curve scenarios later prove HOL-bound, file targeted tasks for those rather than carrying this generic transport task.

Shipped in PR #25 (344fdf8 on main). Firmware DMOVE/UMOVE animated commands + browser per-segment dispatch + linear easing. Hardware testing confirmed firmware works correctly; perceived 'DMOVE ignores duration' was the servo mechanical max-slew rate (~7.7s full sweep). Operator-facing slew-rate docs tracked as servo-ao9.
Documented servo slew-rate floor and motion-authoring guidance
Baked MOTION servo tracks now use 0..100% winch semantics via percentToDegrees/degreesToPulse; host motion tests updated and passing; schema and README aligned.

Closing per operator decision; remaining acceptance clauses (lzn.1 automated test harness, lzn.5 full hardware guardrail matrix beyond the recorded servo-ao9 slew observation) accepted as-is.

Implemented bake timeout recovery retries for /sequences/info with backoff and documented host test coverage.

Identified likely mismatch: WiFi reconnect restored router presence but firmware did not restart HTTP/OTA/UDP listeners. Firmware now restarts network services after reconnect and keeps WiFi recovery running after USB-only boot; hardware reconnect smoke remains documented.
Closed

Merged to main (PR #38, commit 6113918). RUN AUTO setlist scheduler shipped: leader-gated ordered/shuffle scheduling, per-entry repeat/gapMs, rides the RUN/STOP mirror. Host tests 10/10; on-hardware (board 1) verified: scheduler order/repeat/3s-gap (via serial timing), clean STOP with no self-resume, and leader-gate (follower no-ops on direct RUN AUTO). Deferred to follow-up tasks: cluster-follow verification (needs motors on boards 2/3) and on-hardware shuffle run; avoidSameTag/moodArc (servo-yxd).

Verified complete in main: (1) board-card online status uses OFFLINE_AFTER_MS consistently (servo_controller.html:2308, no stray 4000 threshold); (2) WAVE pill fully removed from renderPills (no wave/s.wave references); (3) telemetry renders 3 servo meters + 1 DC with signed speed + ramp indicator ('Actuators · 3 servo · 1 dc'). All three acceptance criteria met; was left in_progress since 2026-05-24.

Merged to main (PR #37) + step-palette follow-up (#39). Removed legacy PLAY/SPLAY/RUN-n engine + TIMESCALE (firmware + webpage controls); reclaimed ~5.1KB OTA (120200→115076). Host suites green; on-hardware legacy-regression (Part 1, board 1) verified 2026-06-01: surviving commands work, removed ones no-op gracefully, // 02 cards render. Done.

Merged to main (PR #41). Editable command combobox (datalist suggests baked motions; free-type any command) + hover command help; fixed the closed-<select> data-loss bug (custom commands like 'ROTATE 24' now survive save/reload). Verified in preview. Done.

Merged to main. Firmware GET /sequences + browser Pull-from-Boards hydration (manual-only per operator decision) with per-board reconcile + source-of-truth conflict pick; fixed latent normalizeKeyframes crash on sliced motions. Host/preview verified; on-hardware endpoint read-back (case 1, board 1) confirmed 3618B==bytesUsed, schemaVersion 1, sliced track boardIds. Feature shipped + working; remaining TESTING.md manual cases (204 empty board, pull-rebuild on live cluster, conflict pick) are optional verification.

Removed the stray /issues.jsonl (stale duplicate export, last written 2026-05-26) and added it to .gitignore. Canonical beads export remains .beads/issues.jsonl; bd no longer writes to root, so deletion is sufficient and it can't be re-added. Committed to main.

Shipped in PR #42 (merged to main 2026-06-01). Firmware gallery_mode flag + grace period; host tests 9/9; all 5 manual hardware cases pass on leader over USB. Browser toggle is follow-up servo-9li (now unblocked).

Implemented: /status.json now exposes gallery, masthead toggle reflects reachable boards and sends GALLERY ON/OFF with enable confirmation. Host tests, OTA size guard, JS syntax, and browser preview passed. Physical cluster smoke/reboot verification is tracked separately as servo-82f.

Hardware cluster smoke completed by operator: browser Gallery toggle verified across reachable boards after firmware with /status.json gallery support; ON confirmation, persistent gallery=true/false reporting, reboot persistence, and OFF path accepted.

Fixed and shipped in PR #44 (merged to main 2026-06-01). otaReceive fail-fast (client.connected + idle 5s + cap 120s); /status.json fw marker; ota-all.sh sequential default + per-board retry + url/IP normalize + verify-via-reboot (no more false FAIL). Whole 3-board cluster updated to servo-6lc-ota-2 and verified. Single-board OTA reliable ~8KB/s/15s; parallel OTA crashes the cluster so it is now non-default. Follow-up: .138 chronic reachability is the separate servo-hem.

Shipped in PR #46 (merged to main). Sequence ids now derive from the name (mirroring Motion), Rename ID button rewrites Setlist seqId + RUN <id> refs, pickers lead with name. Browser-only, verified in preview, no console errors.
Completed shared bake_parse.h refactor; motion, sequence, setlist, full host suite, and OTA size guard pass.

Implemented compile-firmware.sh and browser Use compiled bin flow. Verified script syntax, actual arduino-cli compile output, inline controller JS syntax, and local HTTP fetch of controller/manifest/bin.
Resolved by combined browser OTA helper plus servo-e0n delayed-apply firmware; browser loads servo-e0n-1 and hardware upload test completed without manual power-cycle.
Resolved as same OTA receive/apply race: combined browser OTA helper plus servo-e0n delayed-apply firmware kept boards reachable through upload without manual power-cycle.

Fixed: switched synchronized Motion start from absolute shared-clock timestamp to relative (millis()+leadMs per board), and from WiFi broadcast to unicast-per-peer. Verified on 3-board hardware: 100% cross-board delivery, 14/14 trials from every originator, zero instant-completes. Build servo-vna-5.
Removed syncMillis/clockOffset/lowestAlivePeerId and the HB leader-clock-learning; kept peer lastUptimeMs (peers.json) and PEER_ALIVE_MS. Compiles, host tests green.

Merged to main via PR #48 (squash 5b4a5d9). Synchronized cluster Motion start: relative-timing MOTION_START unicast to peers, verified on 3-board hardware (100% delivery, 14/14 trials). Physical alignment/drift check remains in servo-9oh (needs servos on boards 2&3).
Implemented project-folder library.json persistence via local helper server; browser loads/saves file first with localStorage fallback; documented and smoke-tested.
Closed

Superseded by browser-side rework — no firmware change needed.

When filed (2026-05-25), the browser sprayed ~13 STOPs over 1.2s to win the race against ROTATE frames buffered across Chrome's ~6 parallel HTTP/1.1 connections. The issue proposed a firmware-side fix: clear the in-flight HTTP command queue on STOP.

Subsequent browser reworks solved this a different way:
- servo-79q (05-28): servo tracks dispatch per-segment DMOVE instead of streaming per-frame ROTATE — removes the parallel spray that caused the race.
- servo-a1e (05-30): per-board rate-matching/throughput cap — eliminates the ROTATE backlog, leaving at most ~1 fresh ROTATE in the board TCP buffer.
- AbortController cancels in-flight motion fetches on STOP/end so queued ROTATEs never reach the wire.

Acceptance criterion ('STOP spray can drop back to 1-2 sends') is already met: servo_controller.html now sends a single STOP per board (stopMotionPlayback: 'No more spray', line ~3968).

Also note: firmware has no software command queue to clear — webPoll() accepts one client per loop() and runs it synchronously; the only backlog is the WiFiS3 socket buffer, which the browser fix keeps near-empty. Closing as obsolete/superseded.

Part 2 (browser 1-hour setlist simulation) shipped and verified. Parts 1 (on-device dry-run) and 3 (transition log) split to servo-5fj and servo-71i.

Delivered: a device-faithful JS port of the firmware setlist scheduler (SETLIST-SIM-CORE block in servo_controller.html) + a 'Simulate hour' UI in the Setlist section — verdict banner, per-sequence play-balance bars, pacing line, and a representative timeline flagging too-soon repeats / dead air / missing sequences. Seeded setlists are reproducible; unseeded show stats over 100 runs + Re-roll.

Acceptance criterion 'simulation timeline matches device behavior' is PROVEN: test/verify_sim_matches_firmware.mjs asserts the JS picks equal the firmware's exact order on two oracles (gallery seed=42, and a weighted seed=12345 case captured from the C++ scheduler). Analysis layer + a degenerate-cap regression covered by verify_sim_analysis.mjs. Both wired into 'make sim-verify' and 'make test'. Render verified end-to-end against a stub DOM. Interactive/visual smoke tracked in servo-cdz.

Won't do — operator confirmed remote/unattended gallery monitoring isn't part of their workflow. The transitions log's only value is after-the-fact remote visibility; current sequence is already shown live in /status.json, and the servo-6j4 simulation catches bad orderings pre-deploy. No need.

Won't do — pre-deploy validation is already covered by the servo-6j4 browser simulation, and real runs can be watched on the rig directly. The only unique value (verify on-device without moving servos) doesn't justify a firmware change + OTA + size-budget cost. Operator confirmed not needed.

Shipped + manually verified. Alt-drag shape curves (ramp/sine/triangle/square/ease/jitter) generate editable keyframes; servo feasibility enforced via amplitude reduction, square→trapezoid, and fitKeyframesToNeighbors (boundary fix). Reported bug (infeasible 0@0→shape segment) fixed and confirmed in-browser (checklist E: no red segments, 'eased to fit neighbours', bake not blocked). 73 node assertions + full C++ suite green. Follow-ups: servo-vby (time-stretch for full-amplitude servo shapes), servo-p36 (move selection), multi-track apply, sine multi-cycle.
Shipped + verified (checklist F): per-row Clr button resets a track to a single 0@0 keyframe. Toggles column widened to 110px so Clr no longer overlaps the first keyframe.

Shipped + verified (checklist F): shift-drag marquee selects keyframes (green outline), shift-click toggles, Delete/Backspace removes (ignored while typing). Pure helpers keyframesInMarquee/deleteKeyframeIndices unit-tested (9 assertions). Follow-up servo-p36 for moving the selection.

Shipped + verified in browser. DC shapes keep dense keyframes (faithful baked envelope); Play Live throttles the DC stream to the live throughput floor (>=1200ms/setpoint per track) instead of blocking dense DC. Pivoted away from coarsening stored keyframes after realizing baked DC steps instantly, so dense = more faithful and coarsening hurt the gallery's baked path. Baked MOTION runs full-rate. Throttle decimation validated by simulation; shape tests dense; full suite green.

Shipped (#54): simplifyKeyframes collapses redundant shape keyframes per interpolation (servo collinear→corners, dc value-dedup), applied after generate+fit in applyShapeToTrack, so shapes fit the 64-keyframe/board firmware budget. Fixes the 'exceeds firmware max 64' bake block. 43 shape assertions + stub smoke.

Shipped (#55): consistent Play-button language — amber '≋ Play Live' for browser-streamed preview, green '▣ Play'/'▣ Play (baked)' (.cmd.baked) for on-device/baked playback across Motion/board-grid/Sequencer/Setlist, with tooltips. 'MOTION Cmd' renamed to 'Play (baked)'.
